description_en: A cultural property consisting of the bowl-shaped gong (fusegane) of Eirinji Temple. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village.
A cultural property consisting of the nirvana painting (nehanzu) of Eirinji Temple. Village-designated by Ten'ei Village.
A historic site consisting of the Kunikami castle manor. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1973-04-01.
A historic site consisting of the Shimoaragu group of ancient burial mounds (kofun). Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1974-06-07.

name_en: Sanjome Lion Dance and Hiraguwa Dance
description_en: An intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the Sanjome lion dance and hiraguwa dance. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1975-04-15.
A historic site consisting of the Yanaka group of ancient burial mounds (kofun). Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1976-04-15.
A natural monument consisting of the pine tree avenue of Gohonmatsu. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1977-09-07.
An intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the Kumano devotional association (kumanoko) of Harajuku. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1981-05-01. Located at Nakahata.

name_en: Takihachiman Thirty-Three Kannon Cliff-Carved Buddha Group
description_en: A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of the Takihachiman group of thirty-three Kannon cliff-carved buddhas (magaibutsu). Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1981-05-01.
An intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the Tendo nenbutsu dance of Owaku. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 1981-05-01. Located at Owaku and Owauchi.
A tangible cultural property (archaeological material) consisting of the Meishin memorial tower (kuyoto). Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 2004-05-07.
An intangible cultural property (folk performing art) consisting of the Tenno festival drum (taiko) of Nejuku. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 2004-05-07.

name_en: Memorial Tower of Terauchi Amida Hall
description_en: A tangible cultural property (archaeological material) consisting of the memorial tower (kuyoto) of the Terauchi Amida hall. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 2004-05-07.

name_en: Eighty-Four Coffered Ceiling Paintings of Shofukuji Main Hall
description_en: A tangible cultural property (painting) consisting of eighty-four coffered ceiling paintings in the main hall of Shofukuji Temple. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 2004-05-07.
A tangible cultural property (archaeological material) consisting of three memorial towers (kuyoto) at Amidayu. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 2004-05-07. Located at Seki-no-ue.

name_en: Stone Lantern of the Former Mito Highway
description_en: A tangible cultural property (historical material) consisting of one everlasting stone lantern (joyato) of the former Mito Highway. Town-designated by Yabuki Town on 2013-02-15.
A tangible cultural property (old documents) consisting of the Funaki family documents of Ryuzoan. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1985-10-16.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a standing wooden statue of Prince Shotoku. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1985-10-16.

name_en: Standing Stone Merciful-Mother Kannon of Oyanaizu
description_en: A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a standing stone statue of the merciful-mother (hibo) Kannon of Oyanaizu. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1986-10-17.

name_en: Standing Thirty-Three Kannon of Tashiro Kannonyama
description_en: A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of standing statues of the thirty-three Kannon of Tashiro Kannonyama. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1986-01-01.
A natural monument consisting of the pine tree known as Magotaro Matsu. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1987-10-25.

name_en: Paper Hanging Scroll Brushed by Nakano Takeko
description_en: A tangible cultural property (calligraphy/texts) consisting of a paper hanging scroll brushed by Nakano Takeko. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1987-12-20.

name_en: Lacquered Oak Staff Used by Nakano Yuko
description_en: A tangible cultural property (craft object) consisting of the lacquered oak staff favored by Nakano Yuko. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1987-12-20.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated wooden statue of the great king Enma. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1987-12-23.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a wooden statue of Fudo Myoo. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1988-01-01.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated wooden statue of the Monju Bodhisattva. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1988-01-01.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated wooden statue of Yakushi Nyorai. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1998-04-01.

name_en: Excavated Items from the Ishiomae Site (Jadeite Bead)
description_en: A tangible cultural property (archaeological material) consisting of items excavated from the Ishiomae site, including a jadeite bead. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 1998-04-01.
A tangible cultural property (calligraphy/texts) consisting of authentic calligraphy by Rai Mikisaburo. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 2001-07-24.

name_en: Preserved Foods of Yanaizu Town, Okuaizu
description_en: An intangible cultural property consisting of the traditional preserved foods of Yanaizu Town in the Okuaizu region. Town-designated by Yanaizu Town on 2024-10-01.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated wooden statue of Tokuitsu Bodhisattva. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a standing wooden statue of the sacred Kannon. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated wooden statue of the thousand-armed Kannon. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated wooden statue of Yakushi Nyorai. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.
A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated wooden statue of Amida Nyorai. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.

name_romaji: Dainichi Nyorai Zazō oyobi Ryō Wakiji Zō
name_en: Seated Dainichi Nyorai and Two Attendant Statues
description_en: A tangible cultural property (sculpture) consisting of a seated statue of Dainichi Nyorai together with two attendant statues. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.

name_en: Color-on-Silk Mandala of the Two Realms
description_en: A tangible cultural property (painting) consisting of a color-on-silk painting of the Mandala of the Two Realms. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.

name_romaji: Shihon Chakushoku Shingon Hasso Zō Kakejiku
name_en: Color-on-Paper Hanging Scrolls of the Eight Shingon Patriarchs
description_en: A tangible cultural property (painting) consisting of color-on-paper hanging scrolls depicting the eight Shingon patriarchs. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.

name_en: Color-on-Paper Hanging Scrolls of the Twelve Deva
description_en: A tangible cultural property (painting) consisting of color-on-paper hanging scrolls depicting the Twelve Deva (Juniten). Village-designated by Yugawa Village.
A tangible cultural property (craft) known by tradition as the Oizuka mound. Village-designated by Yugawa Village.
